item.title: Best-in-Class 'Starkiller' Phishing Kit Bypasses MFA
item.description:
A user-friendly PhaaS tool beats standard methods for detecting phishing attacks by live-proxying legitimate login sites.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Supply Chain Attack Secretly Installs OpenClaw for Cline Users
item.description:
The malicious version of Cline's npm package — 2.3.0 — was downloaded more than 4,000 times before it was removed.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Emerging Chiplet Designs Spark Fresh Cybersecurity Challenges
item.description:
As scaled-down circuits with limited functions redefine computing for AI systems and autonomous vehicles, their flexibility demands new approaches to safeguard critical infrastructure.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Latin America's Cyber Maturity Lags Threat Landscape
item.description:
The slower pace of upgrades has the unintended impact of creating a haven for attackers, especially for initial access brokers and ransomware gangs.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lessons From AI Hacking: Every Model, Every Layer Is Risky
item.description:
After two years of finding flaws in AI infrastructure, two Wiz researchers advise security pros to worry less about prompt injection and more about vulnerabilities.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 'God-Like' Attack Machines: AI Agents Ignore Security Policies
item.description:
Microsoft Copilot recently summarized and leaked user emails; but any AI agent will go above and beyond to complete assigned tasks, even breaking through their carefully designed guardrails.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Attackers Use New Tool to Scan for React2Shell Exposure
item.description:
Researchers say threat actors wielded the sophisticated — and unfortunately named — toolkit to target high-value networks for React2Shell exploitation.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 600+ FortiGate Devices Hacked by AI-Armed Amateur
item.description:
A Russian-speaking hacker used generative AI to compromise the FortiGate firewalls, targeting credentials and backups for possible follow-on ransomware attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Enigma Cipher Device Still Holds Secrets for Cyber Pros
item.description:
The Nazi relic's history is riddled with resilience errors, and those lessons still apply to defending against modern cyber threats.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran's MuddyWater Targets Orgs With Fresh Malware as Tensions Mount
item.description:
The long-active Iranian threat group debuted various attack strains and payloads in attacks against organizations in the Middle East and Africa.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Spitting Cash: ATM Jackpotting Attacks Surged in 2025
item.description:
The attacks cost banks more than $20 million in losses last year, as criminals used many of the same tools and tactics they have wielded for more than a decade.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical Grandstream Phone Vulnerability Exposes Calls to Interception
item.description:
The flaw tracked as CVE-2026-2329 can be exploited without authentication for remote code execution with root privileges. The post Critical Grandstream Phone Vulnerability Exposes Calls to Interception appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: PayPal Data Breach Led to Fraudulent Transactions
item.description:
PayPal blamed an application error for the exposure of customer personal information for nearly 6 months. The post PayPal Data Breach Led to Fraudulent Transactions appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Mississippi Hospital System Closes All Clinics After Ransomware Attack
item.description:
A ransomware attack forced the University of Mississippi Medical Center to close all of its roughly three dozen clinics around the state and cancel elective procedures. The post Mississippi Hospital System Closes All Clinics After Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Recent RoundCube Webmail Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks
item.description:
Patched in December 2025, the exploited flaw leads to XSS attacks via the animate tags in SVG documents. The post Recent RoundCube Webmail Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hundreds of FortiGate Firewalls Hacked in AI-Powered Attacks: AWS
item.description:
Threat actors relying on AI have been exploiting exposed ports and weak credentials to take over FortiGate devices. The post Hundreds of FortiGate Firewalls Hacked in AI-Powered Attacks: AWS appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Romanian Hacker Pleads Guilty to Selling Access to US State Network
item.description:
Catalin Dragomir admitted in a US court to selling access to an Oregon state government office’s network. The post Romanian Hacker Pleads Guilty to Selling Access to US State Network appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Autonomous AI Agents Provide New Class of Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
While this campaign targets crypto wallets and steals money, the methodology has far wider potential that could be used by other attackers. The post Autonomous AI Agents Provide New Class of Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: US Healthcare Diagnostic Firm Says 140,000 Affected by Data Breach
item.description:
The Everest ransomware group has taken credit for a hacker attack on Vikor Scientific, now called Vanta Diagnostics. The post US Healthcare Diagnostic Firm Says 140,000 Affected by Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ukraine says cyberattacks on energy grid now used to guide missile strikes
item.description:
Russian cyberattacks targeting Ukraine’s energy infrastructure are increasingly focused on collecting intelligence to guide missile strikes rather than immediately disrupting operations, Ukrainian cybersecurity officials said.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Global data protection authorities warn generative AI companies against replicating real people
item.description:
The joint statement comes on the heels of the Grok AI chatbot creating and sharing millions of images of “nudified” real people.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Air Côte d'Ivoire confirms cyberattack following ransomware claims
item.description:
Air Côte d'Ivoire did not respond to requests for comment but released a statement on Friday confirming reports that hackers had breached its systems on February 8. Last week, the INC ransomware gang claimed it stole 208 GB of data from the airline.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russian-speaking hackers used gen AI tools to compromise 600 firewalls, Amazon says
item.description:
A Russian-speaking threat actor used commercial generative artificial intelligence tools to help compromise more than 600 FortiGate firewall devices across more than 55 countries earlier this year, researchers have found.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Happy 16th Birthday, KrebsOnSecurity.com!
item.description:
KrebsOnSecurity.com celebrates its 16th anniversary today! A huge "thank you" to all of our readers -- newcomers, long-timers and drive-by critics alike. Your engagement this past year here has been tremendous and truly a salve on a handful of dark days. Happily, comeuppance was a strong theme running through our coverage in 2025, with a primary focus on entities that enabled complex and globally-dispersed cybercrime services.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: TWU Local 100
item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to QILIN have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: QILIN | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Silver Lake Medical Center
item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to THEGENTLEMEN have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: THEGENTLEMEN | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: A single compromised account gave hackers access to 1.2 million French banking records
item.description:
Emma Woollacott reports: Credentials stolen from a single government official enabled threat actors to access a French national database containing data on more than 1.2 million bank accounts. The attackers were able to access the Fichier des Comptes Bancaires et Assimilés (FICOBA) database, which contains files on all bank accounts opened in France. Stolen credentials... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Romanian hacker faces up to 7 years for breaching Oregon emergency management department
item.description:
Jonathan Greig reports: A 45-year-old Romanian national pleaded guilty this week to hacking into computers at Oregon’s Department of Emergency Management in June 2021 and selling the access he obtained for $3,000 worth of Bitcoin. Catalin Dragomir also hacked into 10 other U.S. companies, causing financial losses of at least $250,000. He was arrested in... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Resource: Privacy Law Directory — Codamail
item.description:
Regular readers of my companion privacy-oriented site, PogoWasRight.org, may recall that the site recently noted The Data Broker Directory: Who has your data, where they got it, and who they sell it to by Codamail’s Stephen K. Gielda of Packetderm. Instead of taking a well-deserved break after all the work he did to compile that... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Hospitals at Risk of BeyondTrust Ransomware Hacks
item.description:
Marianne Kolbasuk McGee reports: U.S. federal authorities and industry officials are urging hospitals and clinics to address a critical flaw in BeyondTrust Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access software, which if exploited, could give an attacker a foothold inside a corporate network. The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services in an alert Thursday warned... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Update Chrome now: Zero-day bug allows code execution via malicious webpages
item.description:
Pieter Arntz reports: Google has issued a patch for a high‑severity Chrome zero‑day, tracked as CVE‑2026‑2441, a memory bug in how the browser handles certain font features that attackers are already exploiting. CVE-2026-2441 has the questionable honor of being the first Chrome zero-day of 2026. Google considered it serious enough to issue a separate update of the stable... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Discord’s age verification data has a frontend leak — now what?
item.description:
Interesting Engineering reports: A newly uncovered flaw in Discord’s age verification rollout has added fresh pressure to the company’s 2026 compliance plans. Security researchers recently found that frontend components tied to identity vendor Persona were accessible on the open web, prompting debate over how securely the platform handles sensitive age checks. The discovery surfaced on... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: This is how you do it: Dentist speaks out after practice hit by cyber attack
item.description:
This could be the smallest breach DataBreaches has reported recently, and yet we are covering it instead of other, much bigger breaches that will undoubtedly generate lots of headlines. Why? Because it represents a refreshing example of quick response and transparency. Dr. Joe McEnhill, owner of Grange Dental Care, said the breach occurred on Thursday... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Ukrainian hackers uncover how Russian drone operators are using Belarus
item.description:
As seen on InformNapalm: On February 18, 2026, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy imposed sanctions against Belarusian dictator Alyaksandr Lukashenka for his role in escalating and prolonging Russia’s aggressive war against Ukraine. One of the stated reasons is that in the second half of 2025, Russia deployed a system of repeaters for the control of attack... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: The hospitality sector continues to be lucrative targets
item.description:
The hospitality sector has long been a target of hackers, and it’s a global problem. Here are three recent attacks in the news this week: In the U.S.: Choice Hotels International disclosed a breach affecting franchisees and applicants. Its notification letter states that a “skilled person used social engineering” to gain access on January 14,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Top NATO allies believe cyberattacks on hospitals are an act of war. They’re still struggling to fight back.
item.description:
Maggie Miller, Dana Nickel and Antoaneta Roussi report: NATO countries’ restrained response to hybrid attacks is at odds with public opinion, new polling shows: Broad swaths of the public in key allied countries say actions such as cyberattacks on hospitals should be considered acts of war. The POLITICO Poll, conducted in the United States, Canada,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Report Reveals Elevenfold Increase in Data-only Extortion Attacks
item.description:
There has been a sharp increase in data-only extortion incidents, with ransomware gangs increasingly opting not to encrypt files, instead […] The post Report Reveals Elevenfold Increase in Data-only Extortion Attacks appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Top of the World Treatment Center Settles Alleged Risk Analysis HIPAA Violation
item.description:
The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) has announced its first financial penalty of […] The post Top of the World Treatment Center Settles Alleged Risk Analysis HIPAA Violation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Cyberattacks Announced by WIRX Pharmacy and Emanuel Medical Center
item.description:
WIRX Pharmacy in Pennsylvania has experienced a security incident that exposed the protected health information of more than 20,000 current […] The post Cyberattacks Announced by WIRX Pharmacy and Emanuel Medical Center appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Granite Wellness Centers & Pediatric Home Service Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits
item.description:
Granite Wellness Centers in California and Pediatric Home Service in Minnesota have both settled lawsuits stemming from cyberattacks that exposed […] The post Granite Wellness Centers & Pediatric Home Service Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: UMMC Shuts Clinics While it Grapples with Ransomware Attack
item.description:
University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) has temporarily closed most of its clinics following a ransomware attack, and scheduled appointments […] The post UMMC Shuts Clinics While it Grapples with Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Why Healthcare Staff Need HIPAA Training for Social Media
item.description:
Healthcare staff need HIPAA training for social media because a single post, photo, or comment can expose Protected Health Information […] The post Why Healthcare Staff Need HIPAA Training for Social Media appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Vikor Scientific Affected by Ransomware Attack on Revenue Cycle Management Vendor
item.description:
Vikor Scientific (now rebranded as Vanta Diagnostics), a molecular diagnostics company based in Charleston, South Carolina, has been affected by […] The post Vikor Scientific Affected by Ransomware Attack on Revenue Cycle Management Vendor appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Mississippi medical center closes all clinics after ransomware attack
item.description:
The University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) closed all its clinic locations statewide on Thursday following a ransomware attack. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: PayPal discloses data breach that exposed user info for 6 months
item.description:
PayPal is notifying customers of a data breach after a software error in a loan application exposed their sensitive personal information, including Social Security numbers, for nearly 6 months last year. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Data breach at French bank registry impacts 1.2 million accounts
item.description:
The French Ministry of Finance has published an announcement informing of a cybersecurity incident that has impacted 1.2 million accounts. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: CISA: BeyondTrust RCE flaw now exploited in ransomware attacks
item.description:
Hackers are actively exploiting the CVE-2026-1731 vulnerability in the BeyondTrust Remote Support product, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warns. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Japanese tech giant Advantest hit by ransomware attack
item.description:
Advantest Corporation disclosed that its corporate network has been targeted in a ransomware attack that may have affected customer or employee data. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Amazon: AI-assisted hacker breached 600 Fortinet firewalls in 5 weeks
item.description:
Amazon is warning that a Russian-speaking hacker used multiple generative AI services as part of a campaign that breached more than 600 FortiGate firewalls across 55 countries in five weeks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: CISA: Recently patched RoundCube flaws now exploited in attacks
item.description:
CISA flagged two Roundcube Webmail vulnerabilities as actively exploited in attacks and ordered U.S. federal agencies to patch them within three weeks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Ad tech firm Optimizely confirms data breach after vishing attack
item.description:
New York-based ad tech company Optimizely has notified an undisclosed number of customers of a data breach after threat actors compromised some of its systems in a voice phishing attack. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Spain arrests suspected hacktivists for DDoSing govt sites
item.description:
Spanish authorities have arrested four alleged members of a hacktivist group believed to have carried out cyberattacks targeting government ministries, political parties, and various public institutions. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

Cymot reported a ransomware. Attributed to Qilin.
